arrest rights.intro: A person has certain legal protections during arrest.
rights_list: You have the right to know why you are being arrested.
You have the right to remain silent.
You have the right to contact a lawyer.
You have the right to inform a family member or friend.
You should be produced before a magistrate within 24 hours.
important_notes: Police should not use unnecessary force.
Women should generally not be arrested at night except under special legal procedure.
Ask for the identity of the police officer if needed.
arrest rights.next_step: Stay calm, ask the reason for arrest, and contact a lawyer immediately.

women rights.intro: Women are protected under several legal safeguards.
rights_list: You can file complaints against harassment or domestic violence.
You have the right to police protection in certain cases.
You can seek legal aid and support services.
You have the right to dignity and privacy during investigation.
You can seek protection against workplace harassment.
important_notes: A woman can request that her statement be recorded by a woman officer in some situations.
Legal remedies are available for workplace and domestic abuse.
Identity protection may apply in sensitive cases.
women rights.next_step: Approach the nearest police station, women helpline, or legal aid center.

consumer rights.intro: Consumers have rights against unfair trade and defective products.
rights_list: You have the right to safety.
You have the right to be informed.
You have the right to seek redressal.
You have the right to complain about defective goods or poor services.
You have the right to compensation in proper cases.
important_notes: Keep bills, receipts, and product details safe.
You can file a complaint before the consumer commission.
Written proof strengthens your complaint.
consumer rights.next_step: Collect proof like invoice or bill and file a complaint.

cyber rights.intro: Victims of online fraud or abuse have legal protections.
rights_list: You can report cyber fraud or harassment.
You can preserve screenshots and digital evidence.
You can seek police or cyber crime support.
You can report fake profiles, hacking, and identity theft.
You have the right to seek action against online threats and abuse.
important_notes: Do not delete important messages or screenshots.
Report fraud quickly to improve chances of action.
Save transaction details, phone numbers, and email records.
cyber rights.next_step: File a cyber complaint with all available evidence.

bail rights.intro: Bail allows temporary release from custody depending on the nature of the offense.
rights_list: You can apply for bail through legal procedure.
You have the right to legal representation.
In bailable offenses, bail may be available as a matter of right.
In non-bailable offenses, the court decides based on the facts and law.
important_notes: The court may impose conditions while granting bail.
Violation of bail conditions may lead to cancellation.
Quick legal advice is very important.
bail rights.next_step: Contact a lawyer immediately and file the correct bail application.

fir rights.intro: An FIR is an important first step in criminal procedure for cognizable offenses.
rights_list: You have the right to file an FIR.
Police must register FIR for cognizable offenses.
You can receive a copy of the FIR free of cost.
You can approach higher officers if police refuse to register the FIR.
important_notes: Give clear facts with date, time, and place.
Attach available proof if possible.
Keep the FIR copy or complaint acknowledgement safe.
fir rights.next_step: Visit the police station, explain the facts clearly, and collect a copy of the FIR or complaint.

domestic violence rights.intro: Victims of domestic violence are protected against physical, emotional, verbal, and economic abuse.
rights_list: You have the right to protection from abuse.
You have the right to seek legal help and police support.
You may seek shelter and protection orders.
You may seek residence-related protection in appropriate cases.
important_notes: Keep photos, medical proof, messages, and witness details safe.
Personal safety is the first priority.
Protection orders can be requested through legal process.
domestic violence rights.next_step: Move to a safe place if needed, contact police or helpline, and seek legal protection immediately.

child rights.intro: Children are protected under special laws for education, safety, and welfare.
rights_list: Children have the right to education.
Children have the right to protection from child labor.
Children have the right to protection from abuse and exploitation.
Children have the right to dignity, care, and legal protection.
important_notes: Child abuse should be reported immediately.
Schools and guardians have responsibilities for child safety.
Child welfare authorities can intervene when needed.
child rights.next_step: Report the matter to police, child helpline, school authority, or child welfare officials immediately.

property rights.intro: Property owners have legal rights over ownership, possession, and lawful transfer of property.
rights_list: You have the right to lawful ownership.
You have the right against illegal possession or encroachment.
You have the right to transfer or inherit property legally.
You have the right to seek legal remedy in property disputes.
important_notes: Keep sale deed, patta, tax receipts, and registration records safe.
Always verify title documents before purchase.
Property disputes often require proper document proof.
property rights.next_step: Collect your ownership records and consult a legal expert for the correct civil remedy.

employment rights.intro: Employees and workers have rights related to wages, safety, dignity, and fair treatment at work.
rights_list: You have the right to fair wages or salary.
You have the right to safe working conditions.
You have the right to protection from workplace harassment.
You have the right to seek remedy against wrongful treatment.
important_notes: Keep appointment letters, salary slips, emails, and other work proof safe.
Some complaints should first be raised internally.
Labor office or legal forums may help in serious disputes.
employment rights.next_step: Collect proof of employment and raise the issue before HR, labor authorities, or legal forum.

senior citizen rights.intro: Senior citizens are protected by law and may claim support, dignity, and protection from abuse or neglect.
rights_list: Senior citizens have the right to maintenance and support.
They have the right to dignity and safety.
They can seek legal protection from neglect or abuse.
They may approach authorities for relief when needed.
important_notes: Elder abuse or abandonment should be reported.
Medical and financial records may support the case.
Maintenance claims may be filed under relevant law.
senior citizen rights.next_step: Approach the proper authority, police, or legal aid service for protection and maintenance relief.

education rights.intro: Students and children have rights relating to education, fairness, and equal opportunity.
rights_list: Children have the right to education.
Students should not face unfair discrimination.
Students may seek grievance redressal for unfair treatment.
Educational institutions must follow lawful procedures.
important_notes: Keep admission records, fee receipts, notices, and communication safely.
Write complaints clearly and keep copies.
Approach school, college, board, or education authority when needed.
education rights.next_step: Collect records and raise the matter with the institution or the proper education authority.

tenant rights.intro: Tenants have legal protections relating to residence, rent, and eviction procedure.
rights_list: You have the right to fair treatment by the landlord.
You may have protection against unlawful eviction.
You have the right to basic enjoyment of rented property.
You can seek legal remedy in rent or tenancy disputes.
important_notes: Keep rental agreement, rent receipts, and payment proof safe.
Do not rely only on verbal arrangements.
Eviction and rent disputes often depend on documents and local law.
tenant rights.next_step: Collect your rent agreement and payment proof and consult a legal expert if the dispute becomes serious.

medical rights.intro: Patients have rights relating to treatment, dignity, and access to medical information.
rights_list: You have the right to receive basic information about treatment.
You have the right to dignity and respectful care.
You may seek records and documents related to treatment.
You can complain against serious negligence or unfair medical conduct.
important_notes: Keep prescriptions, reports, bills, and discharge summaries safe.
Write down dates, doctor names, and hospital details.
Medical negligence issues often need records and expert review.
medical rights.next_step: Collect all medical records and approach the hospital authority, grievance body, or legal expert.

general legal rights.intro: Every citizen has certain basic legal rights and protections under law.
rights_list: You have the right to equality before law.
You have the right to legal help.
You have the right to file a complaint.
You have the right to fair treatment.
You have the right to seek remedy before lawful authority.
important_notes: Keep important records and proof safely.
Do not hesitate to approach proper authorities.
Legal advice helps when the issue is serious or urgent.
general legal rights.next_step: Collect your documents and approach the correct authority or legal expert for guidance.
